fullArticle: The fires began with a gentle Midwestern boy who loved animals. Kevin Tubbs grew up in a comfortable suburb outside Omaha, Nebraska, in a canary-yellow ranch house filled with dogs. Tubbs’s mother, a tender-hearted woman given to long spells of depression, had a soft spot for strays. Within the home, the dogs were indulged to an unusual degree, holding status as nearly full members of the family. Once, when Tubbs was thirteen, he became alarmed at how a neighbor was treating his own dog, a Siberian husky. The animal, fizzing with the inexhaustible energy of a creature bred for hauling chockablock toboggans, was confined to a tiny pen, a mere twenty feet square, behind the neighbor’s trailer.
